retest_reason: P2.8' Lens System: --lens security lens retest
retest_summary: Security Scanner lens detected ALL 4 known bugs (KB-1 through KB-4) plus 1 new vulnerability (supercookie). Recall improved from 0% to 100%.

exact_matches.evidence: utils.py:L268-L288 — member path join without normpath/.. prevention
sessions.py:L184, L713, L737 — history assignment without self-reference check
utils.py:L798 — endswith matching instead of exact+subdomain
models.py:L435, L482 — parse_url normalization + urlunparse reconstruction loses //

new_vulnerabilities.description: create_cookie default domain='' creates supercookie sent to ALL domains

analysis.lens_impact: The --lens security prompt's 8-dimension checklist directly enabled detection of all 4 known bugs. The specific guidance — 'check path traversal', 'check proxy matching', 'check URI normalization', 'check object lifecycle' — maps 1:1 to each known bug's root cause. This validates the Lens-as-typed-subagent-prompt model.
analysis.bonus_finding: NEW-1 (supercookie) was NOT on the known bugs list. It's a legitimate security vulnerability discovered by the security lens that existed in the code but wasn't documented in release notes. This demonstrates the lens can find real-world bugs beyond benchmark targets.
analysis.comparison: Original generic subagent (0% recall) focused on code quality patterns (null checks, type hints). Security lens (100% recall) focuses on security-specific patterns. The difference is entirely in the prompt — no code changes to ADD itself.
